project.name: abersetz
project.description: A Python CLI utility that translates entire directories of text and Markdown files using modern AI translation engines, slicing large documents at natural boundaries, translating each chunk, and stitching results back together while preserving the original layout. Supports web-scraping translators, official API services, OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoints, and local models (Hunyuan-MT, TranslateGemma).

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Fire-based command-line interface for abersetz, exposing tr/config/lang/engines/setup/validate/discover subcommands and rendering results via Rich tables.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses python-fire to map a class (AbersetzCLI) and its methods onto CLI subcommands; rich.Table for terminal rendering.

nodes.name: _collect_engine_entries
nodes.summary: Builds the list of EngineEntry rows for the engines command by walking the config's tr/dt/ullm/local engine families and filtering by family, paid status, and configured-only.

nodes.name: _build_options_from_cli
nodes.summary: Translates raw CLI arguments into a validated TranslatorOptions object, normalizing the engine selector, validating language codes, and loading prolog/voc JSON references.

nodes.name: _render_engine_entries
nodes.summary: Renders a Rich table of engine entries showing selector, family, configured status, and API-key requirements.

nodes.name: _render_validation_entries
nodes.summary: Renders a Rich table of engine validation results with success status, latency, and sample translations.

nodes.name: AbersetzCLI
nodes.summary: Root Fire CLI class whose methods (tr, config, lang, engines, setup, validate, discover) define the abersetz command surface.

nodes.name: ConfigCommands
nodes.summary: Nested Fire subcommand group exposing 'config show' and 'config path' for inspecting the loaded configuration.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Defines the TOML-backed configuration model (defaults, credentials, engines) with load/save helpers and credential resolution from env vars or stored values.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen-style dataclasses with custom __setattr__ for selector normalization; uses tomllib for reads and tomli_w for writes.

nodes.name: AbersetzConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level configuration dataclass holding defaults, credentials, and engine configs, with to_dict/from_dict round-trip serialization.

nodes.name: EngineConfig
nodes.summary: Per-engine configuration holding name, chunk sizes, optional credential, and an arbitrary options dict.

nodes.name: Credential
nodes.summary: Credential descriptor supporting either an environment-variable reference or an inline value, with flexible from_any parsing.

nodes.name: Defaults
nodes.summary: Default translation settings (engine, languages, chunk sizes) with selector normalization applied on assignment.

nodes.name: load_config
nodes.summary: Loads the TOML config file, creating a default config on first run and recovering by backing up and regenerating when the file is corrupt.

nodes.name: resolve_credential
nodes.summary: Resolves a credential reference to a concrete secret by looking up named credentials, environment variables, or inline values.

nodes.name: engine_catalog.py
nodes.summary: Central catalog of engine selectors: normalizes and resolves selector strings (e.g. tr/google, dt/deepl) and enumerates available translator and deep-translator providers.

nodes.name: normalize_selector
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes an engine selector string into its normalized family/variant form, handling aliases and casing.

nodes.name: collect_translator_providers
nodes.summary: Returns the available translators-library providers, optionally including paid ones, filtered against the installed pool.

nodes.name: collect_deep_translator_providers
nodes.summary: Returns the available deep-translator providers, de-duplicated and optionally including paid ones.

nodes.name: engines.py
nodes.summary: Engine factory that instantiates the concrete translation engine (translators, deep_translator, lmstudio, LLM, MLX, GGUF) for a given selector and configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Single create_engine dispatch function branching on selector family; uses a local OpenAI-compatible client shim.

nodes.name: create_engine
nodes.summary: Resolves an engine selector and builds the matching engine instance, wiring credentials, model resolution, and runtime options for each provider family.

nodes.name: _build_llm_engine
nodes.summary: Constructs an LlmEngine from a profile, resolving the API credential, base URL, model, and temperature into an OpenAI-compatible client.

nodes.name: openai_lite.py
nodes.summary: Minimal httpx-based OpenAI chat-completions client shim providing just the request/response surface abersetz needs, avoiding the full openai SDK dependency.
nodes.languageNotes: Dataclass response models plus a synchronous httpx.Client mirroring the openai SDK's chat.completions.create shape.

nodes.name: OpenAI
nodes.summary: Lightweight OpenAI client entry point exposing a chat.completions interface against a configurable base URL.

nodes.name: ChatCompletions
nodes.summary: Implements the chat completions create() call, POSTing to the OpenAI-compatible endpoint and parsing the response into typed dataclasses.

nodes.name: setup.py
nodes.summary: Interactive setup wizard that discovers available providers from environment variables, tests their endpoints, and generates a working abersetz configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Rich Progress/Table-driven wizard; probes provider endpoints over httpx and writes a generated AbersetzConfig.

nodes.name: SetupWizard
nodes.summary: Drives the end-to-end setup flow: discover providers, test endpoints, display results, generate config, and validate the result.

nodes.name: DiscoveredProvider
nodes.summary: Data record describing a provider discovered during setup, including availability, model count, engine names, and pricing hints.

nodes.name: setup_command
nodes.summary: Entry function invoked by the CLI setup subcommand that constructs and runs the SetupWizard, exiting non-zero on failure.

nodes.name: validation.py
nodes.summary: Validates configured engines by performing a small live sample translation against each selector and reporting success, latency, and errors.

nodes.name: validate_engines
nodes.summary: Runs a sample translation through each selected engine via an injected create_engine factory, timing each call and collecting ValidationResult records.

nodes.name: _selectors_from_config
nodes.summary: Derives the set of engine selectors to validate from the config's engine families and provider option lists.

nodes.name: _extract_providers
nodes.summary: Extracts and normalizes a sorted list of provider names from an engine options dict, supporting both list and scalar fallback keys.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the translation providers, re-exporting the base engine types and every concrete engine implementation (deep_translator, gguf, llm, lmstudio, mlx, translators).
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ acting as a re-export barrel for the provider engines.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the shared engine contract: the EngineError exception, EngineRequest/EngineResult data structures, the Engine protocol, and the EngineBase class with chunk-size resolution used by all concrete providers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a Protocol-style Engine contract plus dataclasses for request/result payloads.

nodes.name: deep_translator.py
nodes.summary: Engine wrapping the deep-translator library, resolving language codes against provider-supported languages with langcodes fallback and translating text with retry.

nodes.name: gguf.py
nodes.summary: Local GGUF translation engine that downloads/loads a llama.cpp model and runs chat-completion translation using the shared mthy prompt builder.

nodes.name: lmstudio.py
nodes.summary: Engine driving a local LM Studio model, ensuring the lmstudio daemon is running before invoking the loaded model for translation.

nodes.name: mlx.py
nodes.summary: Apple MLX translation engine plus shared model-resolution helpers: language mapping, mthy prompt construction, local model discovery, and Hugging Face download fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest provider file; mixes module-level helper functions (reused by the gguf engine) with the LocalMlxEngine class.

nodes.name: translators.py
nodes.summary: Engine wrapping the translators library, dispatching HTML or plain-text translation with retry handling.

nodes.name: EngineRequest
nodes.summary: Data structure carrying a translation request: text, source/target languages, HTML flag, vocabulary, prolog, and chunk indices.

nodes.name: Engine
nodes.summary: Protocol defining the translation engine interface with translate and chunk_size_for plus name and chunk-size attributes.

nodes.name: EngineBase
nodes.summary: Base class for concrete engines providing common initialization and chunk-size resolution shared across providers.

nodes.name: DeepTranslatorEngine
nodes.summary: Engine adapter for the deep-translator library, resolving language codes to provider-supported forms and translating text with retry logic.

nodes.name: LocalGgufEngine
nodes.summary: Loads a local GGUF model via llama.cpp and translates text through chat completion using the mthy prompt.

nodes.name: LmstudioEngine
nodes.summary: Manages the LM Studio daemon lifecycle and invokes a loaded model to translate text per target language.

nodes.name: find_local_model_path
nodes.summary: Discovers a locally cached model directory matching a model identifier across several naming conventions.

nodes.name: resolve_and_download_model
nodes.summary: Resolves a model name or path to a local directory, applying aliases and falling back to Hugging Face snapshot/file download when not cached.

nodes.name: build_mthy_prompt
nodes.summary: Builds the mthy translation prompt from source text, target language, and optional vocabulary mapping. Reused by the gguf engine.

nodes.name: LocalMlxEngine
nodes.summary: Apple MLX translation engine that loads a local model and generates translations via chat-templated prompts.

nodes.name: TranslatorsEngine
nodes.summary: Engine adapter for the translators library, dispatching HTML or plain-text translation with retry handling.

nodes.name: abersetz.py
nodes.summary: Thin public API module that re-exports the translation pipeline entry points for library consumers.

nodes.name: chunking.py
nodes.summary: Splits source text into translation-sized chunks, detecting HTML vs plain text and using semantic splitting with a length-based fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an enum (TextFormat) plus a semantic TextSplitter with a manual range-based fallback.

nodes.name: pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Core translation orchestration: discovers input files, merges options with config defaults, chunks text (incl. HTML extract/merge), invokes the selected engine with caching, and persists translated output plus vocabulary.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines frozen dataclass option/result types, an LRU-cached engine call, and BeautifulSoup-based HTML splitting.

nodes.name: tasks.py
nodes.summary: Higher-level convenience wrappers around translate_path that build TranslatorOptions and return simplified result mappings.

nodes.name: TextFormat
nodes.summary: Enum distinguishing plaintext, Markdown, and HTML so the chunker can pick an appropriate splitting strategy.

nodes.name: chunk_text
nodes.summary: Public entry that returns a list of text chunks sized to max_size for a given format, delegating to the semantic chunker.

nodes.name: _semantic_chunks
nodes.summary: Splits text into semantically coherent chunks using a TextSplitter, falling back to fixed-size slicing when unavailable.

nodes.name: TranslatorOptions
nodes.summary: Configuration dataclass capturing all translation parameters: engine selection, languages, chunk sizes, output handling, vocabulary, and model runtime options.

nodes.name: TranslationResult
nodes.summary: Result dataclass describing a translated file: source/destination paths, chunk count, vocabulary, format, engine, and language pair.

nodes.name: PipelineError
nodes.summary: Exception raised for pipeline-level failures such as missing paths or empty file selections.

nodes.name: translate_path
nodes.summary: Top-level driver that resolves the input path, loads config, discovers files, instantiates the chosen engine, and translates each file into a list of TranslationResult.

nodes.name: _merge_defaults
nodes.summary: Fills unspecified TranslatorOptions fields from configured defaults and normalizes the engine selector.

nodes.name: _discover_files
nodes.summary: Resolves include glob patterns against the root directory, filtering out excluded paths, and returns a sorted file list.

nodes.name: _translate_file
nodes.summary: Translates a single file end to end: detects format, routes HTML separately, chunks the text, applies the engine, and persists the translated output.

nodes.name: _translate_html
nodes.summary: Handles HTML translation by extracting body elements with BeautifulSoup, grouping them into size-bounded chunks, translating, and merging the result back.

nodes.name: _cached_translate_call
nodes.summary: LRU-cached wrapper that builds an EngineRequest and invokes the engine's translate method, keyed on text and translation parameters.

nodes.name: _apply_engine
nodes.summary: Iterates chunks through the translation engine, threading vocabulary and prolog context between calls and collecting EngineResult objects.

nodes.name: _build_request
nodes.summary: Constructs an EngineRequest for a single chunk with index/total metadata, format, language, and vocabulary context.

nodes.name: _select_chunk_size
nodes.summary: Determines the chunk size for a format by combining engine-derived sizing with user-provided overrides.

nodes.name: _persist_output
nodes.summary: Writes translated content to the destination path (creating directories as needed) and optionally saves the accumulated vocabulary as JSON.

nodes.name: translate_flow
nodes.summary: Convenience flow that builds TranslatorOptions from simple arguments, runs translate_task, and returns simplified source/destination mappings.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the LLM provider, re-exporting endpoint discovery helpers and the LlmEngine inference class as the public surface of the llm subpackage.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ acting as a re-export barrel.

nodes.name: discovery.py
nodes.summary: Discovers OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoints from environment variables and a static catalog, fetches model lists over HTTP, and resolves provider/model selectors including wildcard patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a frozen dataclass Endpoint plus fnmatch for wildcard model selection and httpx for live model fetching.

nodes.name: inference.py
nodes.summary: Implements LlmEngine, an OpenAI chat-completions translation engine that builds prompts, invokes the model, and parses the structured text/vocabulary payload from the response.

nodes.name: Endpoint
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing an OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint with its name, base URL, API-key environment variable, and any known models.

nodes.name: discover_env_endpoints
nodes.summary: Scans environment variables for provider API-key suffixes and constructs Endpoint instances for each discovered provider.

nodes.name: fetch_models
nodes.summary: Queries an endpoint's /models API over HTTP and normalizes the response into a list of model identifier strings.

nodes.name: resolve_model
nodes.summary: Resolves a provider/model selector string against discovered endpoints, handling explicit provider prefixes, defaults, and wildcard patterns.

nodes.name: resolve_wildcard_to_model
nodes.summary: Expands a wildcard model pattern against an endpoint's known and fetched models, returning the first matching model name.

nodes.name: load_recommended_settings
nodes.summary: Loads recommended per-provider engine settings from a bundled JSON catalog file.

nodes.name: LlmEngine
nodes.summary: Translation engine backed by an OpenAI-compatible chat-completions client; builds translation prompts with optional vocabulary, invokes the model, and parses the structured text and vocabulary from the response.

nodes.name: push.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow triggered on pushes/PRs to main: runs Ruff lint and format checks, the pytest suite across Python 3.10-3.12, and builds distribution artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Three sequential jobs (quality -> test -> build) chained via needs; the test job uses a matrix over Python versions and uv for dependency management.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions release workflow triggered on version tags (v*): builds distributions, verifies wheel/sdist presence, publishes to PyPI via trusted publishing, and creates a GitHub Release.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses OIDC id-token permission with a pypi environment for trusted publishing and softprops/action-gh-release for release notes.

nodes.name: quality
nodes.summary: Code Quality job that runs Ruff lint (check) and Ruff format-check against the repository.

nodes.name: test
nodes.summary: Run Tests job that installs the package with test extras and executes pytest with coverage across a Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 matrix.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Build Distribution job that builds sdist and wheel artifacts via python -m build and uploads them.

nodes.name: release
nodes.summary: Release to PyPI job that builds, verifies, publishes distributions to PyPI, and creates a GitHub Release.

nodes.name: .pre-commit-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Pre-commit hook configuration wiring ruff linting and formatting into the git commit workflow.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: AI-assistant development guidelines covering chain-of-thought reasoning, verification workflow, anti-bloat rules, and project conventions for agents working on abersetz.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Accumulative release notes documenting feature additions, fixes, and version history of abersetz.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude-specific development guidelines, near-identical to AGENTS.md, defining CoT reasoning, verification, and anti-overcomplication rules.

nodes.name: DEPENDENCIES.md
nodes.summary: Lists the project's package dependencies with rationale for why each was chosen.

nodes.name: GEMINI.md
nodes.summary: Gemini-specific development guidelines, near-identical to AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, defining AI collaboration conventions.

nodes.name: IDEA.md
nodes.summary: Conceptual notes and original idea sketch describing the motivation and high-level design of abersetz.

nodes.name: LLXPRT.md
nodes.summary: LLxprt-specific development guidelines, near-identical to AGENTS.md, defining AI collaboration conventions.

nodes.name: QWEN.md
nodes.summary: Qwen-specific development guidelines, near-identical to AGENTS.md, defining AI collaboration conventions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview explaining what abersetz does, with usage examples for translating directories of text and Markdown files via AI engines.

nodes.name: SPEC.md
nodes.summary: Technical specification describing the architecture, pipeline stages, and engine interfaces of abersetz.

nodes.name: TASKS.md
nodes.summary: Detailed future goals and planned work for abersetz with specific implementation specifics.

nodes.name: TESTING.md
nodes.summary: Testing strategy and instructions covering unit, integration, and functional example-based tests.

nodes.name: TODO.md
nodes.summary: Flat itemized checklist representation of the TASKS.md roadmap.

nodes.name: WORK.md
nodes.summary: Work progress log capturing iteration notes, test results, and next steps across the development of abersetz.

nodes.name: package.toml
nodes.summary: Project scaffolding configuration toggling CLI, logging, Pydantic, Rich, VCS, and GitHub Actions features.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project manifest defining build system (hatch-vcs), dependencies, CLI entry point, and tool configuration (ruff, pytest, mypy).

nodes.name: build.sh
nodes.summary: Build script that lints, formats, runs tests, and builds the abersetz package via uv/hatch.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: Installation script performing an editable local install of abersetz via uv pip.

nodes.name: publish.sh
nodes.summary: Release script that cleans builds, runs build.sh and install.sh, tags the next version, and publishes the package to PyPI.

nodes.name: _config.yml
nodes.summary: Jekyll site configuration for the abersetz GitHub Pages documentation, using the just-the-docs remote theme with search and navigation enabled.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses remote_theme (just-the-docs) rather than a vendored theme, keeping the docs repo lean.

nodes.name: api.md
nodes.summary: Python API reference documenting the programmatic translation interface, primarily the translate_path entry point and supporting types from the abersetz package.

nodes.name: cli.md
nodes.summary: CLI reference describing the abersetz and abtr commands and subcommands (tr, validate, config, engines, version) exposed by the command-line interface.

nodes.name: configuration.md
nodes.summary: Configuration guide explaining the TOML config file managed by platformdirs, its structure, defaults, and engine settings.

nodes.name: index.md
nodes.summary: Documentation home page introducing abersetz, its value proposition, and getting-started entry points for the documentation site.

nodes.name: installation.md
nodes.summary: Installation guide covering requirements (Python 3.10+) and installation via pip or uv package managers.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Usage guide for the translation engine benchmark suite, documenting how to run examples/benchmark.py with its --engines, --dry-run, --force and related options, and describing the poem and FontLab test documents.

nodes.name: benchmark_results.json
nodes.summary: Persisted machine-readable benchmark output produced by examples/benchmark.py, recording per-engine translation speed, throughput, and success/failure metrics across the test documents.

nodes.name: benchmark_results.md
nodes.summary: Narrative analysis of the translation engine benchmark, comparing local MLX vs GGUF backends, model sizes, and cataloguing failure modes (API length limits, volume/path errors, network timeouts, LM Studio connectivity).

nodes.name: fontlab-7-tldr.en.md
nodes.summary: English source fixture: a 20-chapter FontLab 7 overview used as sample input for the translation pipeline's example/benchmark runs.

nodes.name: fontlab-7-tldr.pl--dt-google.md
nodes.summary: Polish translation of the FontLab 7 overview produced via the deep-translator Google engine, kept as an example output fixture.

nodes.name: fontlab-7-tldr.pl--mthy-mlx-1.8b-mlx.md
nodes.summary: Polish translation of the FontLab 7 overview produced by the local MLX 1.8b model engine, kept as an example output fixture.

nodes.name: fontlab-7-tldr.pl--tr-google.md
nodes.summary: Polish translation of the FontLab 7 overview produced via the translators Google engine, kept as an example output fixture.

nodes.name: poem.en.md
nodes.summary: English source poem used as the input fixture for translation examples and tests.

nodes.name: poem.pl--dt-google.md
nodes.summary: Polish translation of the sample poem produced via the deep-translator Google engine, kept as an example output fixture.

nodes.name: poem.pl--mthy-gguf-1.8b-gguf.md
nodes.summary: Polish translation of the sample poem produced via the GGUF 1.8B local model engine, kept as an example output fixture.

nodes.name: poem.pl--mthy-mlx-1.8b-mlx.md
nodes.summary: Polish translation of the sample poem produced via the MLX 1.8B local model engine, kept as an example output fixture.

nodes.name: poem.pl--tr-google.md
nodes.summary: Polish translation of the sample poem produced via the translators (tr) Google engine, kept as an example output fixture.

nodes.name: codegraph.mdc
nodes.summary: Cursor editor rule documenting when and how to use the CodeGraph MCP tools for code intelligence in this repository.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern manifest excluding build artifacts, caches, and vendored files from the understand-anything codebase analysis.

nodes.name: benchmark.py
nodes.summary: Runnable benchmark harness that resolves engine/provider selectors, translates sample documents through abersetz, and reports timing and output statistics in a rich table.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses fire for CLI dispatch and unittest.mock.patch to swap in dummy engines for deterministic timing.

nodes.name: get_engine_descriptor
nodes.summary: Resolves an engine selector against the configuration to build a sanitized human-readable descriptor of provider, model, and profile.

nodes.name: resolve_provider_to_engines
nodes.summary: Expands a provider name into the list of matching configured engine selectors.

nodes.name: BenchmarkRunner
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full benchmark run: selecting engines, translating sample files, capturing timings, and rendering and persisting a results summary.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package root that lazily exposes the public API (translate_path, TranslatorOptions, etc.) via module __getattr__ and resolves the package version.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PEP 562 module-level __getattr__ for lazy imports to keep import time low.

nodes.name: __getattr__
nodes.summary: Module-level lazy attribute resolver that imports public symbols on first access and raises AttributeError otherwise.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Module execution entry point that delegates to the fast CLI main when the package is run with python -m abersetz.

nodes.name: cli_fast.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight CLI front-end that short-circuits --version without importing the heavy CLI module, otherwise delegating to the full Fire-based CLI.

nodes.name: handle_version
nodes.summary: Detects a --version flag, prints the package version via importlib.metadata, and exits without loading the full CLI.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry function that handles the version fast path then defers to the full CLI main.

nodes.name: anthropic.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring Anthropic API base URL, API key env var, and known Claude model identifiers.

nodes.name: deepseek.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring DeepSeek API endpoint, key env var, and known model identifiers.

nodes.name: gemini.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring Google Gemini's OpenAI-compatible endpoint, key env var, and known model identifiers.

nodes.name: groq.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring Groq API endpoint, key env var, and known model identifiers.

nodes.name: lmstudio.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring the local LM Studio OpenAI-compatible endpoint and key settings.

nodes.name: openai.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring OpenAI API base URL, key env var, and known GPT model identifiers.

nodes.name: openrouter.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring OpenRouter aggregation endpoint, key env var, and known model identifiers.

nodes.name: siliconflow.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring SiliconFlow API endpoint, key env var, and known model identifiers.

nodes.name: together.py
nodes.summary: Provider descriptor module declaring Together AI API endpoint, key env var, and known model identifiers.

nodes.name: local_discovery.py
nodes.summary: Discovers locally installed GGUF/MLX models across LM Studio and other app directories, returning structured LocalModel records.

nodes.name: LocalModel
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing a discovered local model with path, name, app, format, and size.

nodes.name: LocalModelFinder
nodes.summary: Scans known local model directories (resolving LM Studio's pointer file) to enumerate GGUF/MLX models with size metadata.

nodes.name: recommended_settings.json
nodes.summary: Per-provider recommended default settings (model, parameters) for OpenAI, SiliconFlow, Groq, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Gemini, plus a default fallback.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest fixtures and autouse setup: mocks the bcache decorator with a hashable-args cache, isolates the config dir to tmp_path, and clears caches between tests.

nodes.name: test_chunking.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for text chunking and format detection, including round-trip preservation and a fallback path when the semantic splitter is unavailable.

nodes.name: test_cli.py
nodes.summary: Extensive CLI test suite covering argument wiring, option building, engine/validation entry collection, config commands, and Fire invocation.

nodes.name: test_config.py
nodes.summary: Tests for configuration loading, defaults, and persistence behavior.

nodes.name: test_engine_catalog.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the engine catalog that enumerates and resolves available translation engines.

nodes.name: test_engines.py
nodes.summary: Large test suite exercising translation engine creation, selection normalization, and provider behavior.

nodes.name: test_examples.py
nodes.summary: Functional tests validating that the bundled example scripts (including the benchmark) run end-to-end.

nodes.name: test_htmladapt_twat.py
nodes.summary: Tests for HTML adaptation handling used during translation of markup content.

nodes.name: test_integration.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests exercising real translation engines (translators, deep-translator, ullm) plus file/HTML/batch translation and network-failure retry behavior.

nodes.name: test_local_discovery.py
nodes.summary: Tests for local model discovery, verifying the model finder scans correctly and resolves local GGUF and MLX situation models.

nodes.name: test_offline.py
nodes.summary: Offline-mode tests confirming dry-run, empty-file handling, imports, and edge-case file processing all work without network access.

nodes.name: test_openai_lite.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the lightweight OpenAI chat-completions client, validating response parsing and HTTP error handling via dummy client stubs.

nodes.name: test_package.py
nodes.summary: Smoke test verifying package-level dynamic attribute access rejects unknown symbols.

nodes.name: test_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive tests for the translation pipeline's translate_path, covering chunk sizing, HTML handling, mixed formats, write modes, dry-run, and large-file warnings.

nodes.name: test_setup.py
nodes.summary: Extensive tests for the setup wizard: provider discovery, config generation, endpoint testing, validation, default-engine selection, and interactive run flow.

nodes.name: test_validation.py
nodes.summary: Tests for engine validation logic, covering result collection, failure handling, selector limits, empty-translation flagging, and selector extraction from config.

nodes.name: _DummyResponse
nodes.summary: Test double simulating an HTTP response with raise_for_status and json methods.

nodes.name: _DummyClient
nodes.summary: Context-manager test double simulating an HTTP client that returns canned post responses.

nodes.name: test_chat_completions_create_parses_response
nodes.summary: Verifies the lite OpenAI client correctly parses a successful chat-completions response payload.

nodes.name: DummyEngine
nodes.summary: Fake translation engine providing chunk_size_for and translate methods for exercising pipeline logic without real backends.

nodes.name: test_translate_path_uses_engine_chunk_size_when_defaults_falsy
nodes.summary: Confirms translate_path falls back to the engine-provided chunk size when default chunk settings are falsy.

nodes.name: test_translate_path_html_uses_engine_chunk_hint
nodes.summary: Verifies HTML translation through translate_path respects the engine's chunk-size hint.

nodes.name: test_local_model_finder_scans_correctly
nodes.summary: Verifies the local model finder scans directories and identifies available local models correctly.

nodes.name: test_resolve_local_situation_model
nodes.summary: Tests resolution of a local GGUF situation model from discovered candidates.

nodes.name: test_resolve_local_mlx_situation_model
nodes.summary: Tests resolution of a local MLX situation model from discovered candidates.

nodes.name: _StubProgress
nodes.summary: Context-manager stub replacing the rich Progress display during setup wizard tests.

nodes.name: test_generate_config_builds_engines
nodes.summary: Verifies setup config generation builds the expected engine entries from discovered providers.

nodes.name: test_setup_wizard_run_interactive_success

layers.name: CLI & Entry Points
layers.description: Fire-based command-line interface, module entry points, and the top-level abersetz facade that users invoke.

layers.name: Translation Pipeline
layers.description: Orchestration of the translation workflow: file traversal, text chunking, task scheduling, and input/output validation.

layers.name: Engines & Providers
layers.description: Translation engine catalog, engine dispatch, and concrete provider backends (deep-translator, gguf, mlx, lmstudio, translators).

layers.name: LLM Provider Adapters
layers.description: LLM-specific discovery, inference, and per-vendor API adapters (anthropic, openai, gemini, groq, deepseek, openrouter, siliconflow, together, lmstudio).

layers.name: Configuration & Setup
layers.description: Runtime configuration, interactive setup, project manifests, build/install/publish scripts, and recommended-settings data.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Pytest-based unit, integration, and CLI test suites covering the abersetz package.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation, agent guides, specs, changelog, and runnable example data.

layers.name: CI/CD
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflows for push validation and release publishing.

tour.title: What abersetz Does
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the project's mission: feed it a folder of text or Markdown files and get back a translated folder. It introduces the core idea of a unified interface over many translation engines (web-scraping translators, official APIs, OpenAI-compatible LLMs, and local models) and the directory-in/directory-out workflow. This page is the map for everything the tour visits next.

tour.title: Command-Line Entry Point
tour.description: When you run `python -m abersetz` or the `abersetz` command, execution begins here. `__main__.py` delegates to `cli_fast.py`, a lightweight front-end that short-circuits `--version` without importing heavy dependencies, then hands off to the full CLI. This lazy-loading split keeps startup fast for common invocations.
tour.languageLesson: Python packages can be made runnable with `python -m <pkg>` by providing a `__main__.py`. Deferring expensive imports until they are actually needed is a common trick to keep CLI startup snappy.

tour.title: The Command Surface
tour.description: `cli.py` is the Fire-based command surface that turns Python functions into CLI subcommands: `tr` (translate), `validate`, `config`, `engines`, and `setup`. It parses user options, calls into the pipeline, and renders results as Rich tables. This is the bridge between what the README promises and the code that delivers it.
tour.languageLesson: The `fire` library auto-generates a command-line interface from ordinary Python functions and classes, so adding a subcommand is as simple as defining a method.

tour.title: Configuration and Credentials
tour.description: `config.py` is the most depended-upon module in the codebase (highest fan-in): nearly every layer reads from the TOML-backed configuration model for defaults, credentials, and per-engine settings. It resolves API keys from environment variables or stored values. Understanding this shared contract clarifies how the CLI options from Step 3 merge with persistent settings.
tour.languageLesson: TOML is the preferred Python config format (used by pyproject.toml too). `platformdirs` resolves the correct per-OS location for the user config file so the tool behaves natively on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

tour.title: Interactive Setup Wizard
tour.description: Building on the config model from Step 4, `setup.py` is the wizard that discovers available providers from environment variables, tests their endpoints live, and writes a working configuration. It is what a first-time user runs to go from zero to a translatable setup, and it explains how credentials discovered here feed every engine.

tour.title: Pipeline Orchestration
tour.description: `pipeline.py` is the orchestration core that turns a path into translated output. It discovers input files, merges per-call options with config defaults, chunks each document, invokes the selected engine with caching, and writes results into a language-named subdirectory while carrying vocabulary forward. This is the spine that connects every other layer; `tasks.py` and `abersetz.py` wrap it as the friendly public API.

tour.title: Splitting Text into Chunks
tour.description: Translation engines have length limits, so `chunking.py` slices large documents at natural sentence and paragraph boundaries before they reach an engine. It detects HTML versus plain text and falls back to length-based splitting when the semantic splitter is unavailable. This is the step that lets abersetz handle whole books, not just snippets, while preserving layout on reassembly.

tour.title: Selecting an Engine
tour.description: Once text is chunked, the pipeline needs a concrete translator. `engine_catalog.py` normalizes and resolves selector strings like `tr/google` or `dt/deepl` and enumerates available providers, while `engines.py` is the factory that instantiates the right engine object for a given selector and config. Together they implement the unified-interface promise from the README.

tour.title: The Engine Contract and Provider Backends
tour.description: `base.py` defines the shared contract every engine honors: the EngineRequest/EngineResult data structures, the Engine protocol, and an EngineBase with chunk-size resolution. The concrete backends each adapt one ecosystem to that contract: deep_translator (official APIs), translators (web scraping), and the local mlx and gguf model engines. Studying base.py first makes every backend read the same way.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `typing.Protocol` enables structural (duck) typing: any class with the right methods satisfies the Engine protocol without explicitly inheriting from it, which keeps each provider decoupled.

tour.title: LLM Adapters and Discovery
tour.description: The `llm` subpackage handles OpenAI-compatible endpoints. `discovery.py` finds endpoints from environment variables and a static catalog and fetches model lists; `inference.py` implements LlmEngine, which builds XML-wrapped prompts, calls the model, and parses the structured text and vocabulary back out. `openai_lite.py` is a minimal httpx client shim that avoids pulling in the full OpenAI SDK. Per-vendor descriptor modules (anthropic, openai, gemini, and friends) just declare endpoints and known models.

tour.title: Validation and Tests
tour.description: `validation.py` performs a small live sample translation against each configured engine and reports success, latency, and errors, backing the `abersetz validate` command. The pytest suite mirrors the architecture: `test_pipeline.py` and `test_chunking.py` cover the orchestration core, `test_engines.py` and `test_engine_catalog.py` cover engine selection, and `test_offline.py` keeps the happy path runnable without network access.

tour.title: Packaging and CI/CD
tour.description: The capstone shows how abersetz ships. `pyproject.toml` declares the build system (hatch-vcs), dependencies, and the CLI entry point. The `push.yml` workflow runs Ruff linting and the pytest matrix across Python 3.10-3.12 on every push, and `release.yml` builds and publishes to PyPI on version tags. This closes the loop from the code you just toured to a published package users can install.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use `on` triggers (push, tag) and parallel `jobs`. A matrix build runs the same test job across multiple Python versions; hatch-vcs derives the package version from git tags so releases stay in sync with source control.
